rationale: Both correctly diagnose the issue as a run-level misconfiguration (default core_model → dummy weights) rather than a real bug, matching the actual 'invalid' resolution and the fix of using --run-level=full_model. X is more thoroughly grounded, citing precise line ranges across more files (run_args.py, test file, runtime.py, stage_config.py, assertions.py) and independently surfaces the PR #4354 detail that matches akshatvishu's actual comment, plus adds concrete completeness (video path check, GPU memory requirements, a forward-looking footgun suggestion). Y reaches the same correct conclusion but with thinner grounding (fewer/looser citations) and ships as a messy, duplicated raw agent report (repeated answer_draft, 'confidence: high' caveats, 'Run report' boilerplate) rather than a clean maintainer-ready answer, hurting completeness/presentation.
